title: Apache Hadoop Segment 05
summary: A large Apache Hadoop development segment covering 318 PRs across 16 dependency layers. This segment spans the full Hadoop ecosystem: HDFS block management (BlockManager, DatanodeManager, FSNamesystem), HDFS Router-Based Federation (RBF), native libhdfspp client tools, YARN ResourceManager capacity scheduling, YARN Router federation, MapReduce framework, hadoop-common IPC/security/filesystem abstractions, and cloud storage connectors (S3A, Azure). Changes include bug fixes, performance optimizations, new YARN federation APIs, HDFS decommissioning improvements, and test infrastructure hardening.
instructionPreview: `/workspace/requirements/` contains every requirement as a separate file. Each file describes one change request. Read them all, implement every one. Order does not matter, but some changes depend on others.
instruction:
You are an autonomous engineer. The repository at /workspace is at an earlier state of a real-world project.

`/workspace/requirements/` contains every requirement as a separate file. Each file describes one change request. Read them all, implement every one. Order does not matter, but some changes depend on others.

moduleDag.description: A large Apache Hadoop development segment covering 318 PRs across 16 dependency layers. This segment spans the full Hadoop ecosystem: HDFS block management (BlockManager, DatanodeManager, FSNamesystem), HDFS Router-Based Federation (RBF), native libhdfspp client tools, YARN ResourceManager capacity scheduling, YARN Router federation, MapReduce framework, hadoop-common IPC/security/filesystem abstractions, and cloud storage connectors (S3A, Azure). Changes include bug fixes, performance optimizations, new YARN federation APIs, HDFS decommissioning improvements, and test infrastructure hardening.

nodes.label: Hadoop Common
nodes.description: Core shared library: IPC Server (15 edge references), security/token delegation (AbstractDelegationTokenSecretManager), filesystem abstractions (ViewFileSystem, FileSystem), configuration, metrics, crypto (zstd decompression), retry policies, and utility classes. 94 PRs covering the widest range of foundational changes.

nodes.label: HDFS Core (NameNode & DataNode)
nodes.description: HDFS server: BlockManager (78 edge refs, most connected), FSNamesystem (66 refs), DatanodeManager (66 refs), DataNode (21 refs), BPServiceActor (10 refs), NameNode, and decommission monitors. 135 PRs -- the most active module -- addressing block management, storage policies, replication, and data node lifecycle.

nodes.label: HDFS Native Client (libhdfspp)
nodes.description: C++ native HDFS client library and CLI tools (CMakeLists.txt with 12 PRs). 30 PRs for build system, tool implementations (cat, chgrp, chmod, chown, stat), and native filesystem operations.

nodes.label: HDFS Router-Based Federation (RBF)
nodes.description: Federation router: RouterRpcClient (36 edge refs), RouterRpcServer, FederationRPCMetrics (15 refs), fairness policy controllers, and mount table management. 37 PRs for federation routing, metrics, and multi-namespace support.

nodes.label: HDFS Client Library
nodes.description: Java HDFS client: DFSClient, distributed filesystem implementation, configuration keys (DFSConfigKeys with 8 PRs), and protocol definitions. 28 PRs for client-side improvements and configuration additions.

nodes.label: YARN ResourceManager
nodes.description: CapacityScheduler (9 PRs), LeafQueue, ParentQueue, AbstractCSQueue, CapacitySchedulerConfiguration, and resource allocation logic. 48 PRs for capacity scheduling, queue management, and resource reservation.

nodes.label: YARN Router (Federation)
nodes.description: FederationClientInterceptor (45 edge refs), FederationInterceptorREST (36 refs), RouterMetrics (21 refs), and RouterYarnClientUtils. 25 PRs adding federated YARN APIs and improving cross-cluster routing.

nodes.label: YARN Server (Other Services)
nodes.description: Timeline service, application history, web proxy, shared caching, and server common utilities. 23 PRs for auxiliary YARN server components.

nodes.label: YARN NodeManager
nodes.description: Container execution, resource localization, health monitoring, and node lifecycle management. 16 PRs for container management and node operations.

nodes.label: YARN Common & Client
nodes.description: YARN shared utilities, service abstractions, resource definitions, and client libraries. 24 PRs for common infrastructure shared by RM, NM, and router.

nodes.label: YARN Applications
nodes.description: Distributed shell and application-level utilities built on YARN. 12 PRs for application framework improvements.

nodes.label: MapReduce
nodes.description: MapReduce client core, application master, job history, examples, and dev support (jdiff API tracking). 21 PRs for MR framework maintenance, task handling, and backward compatibility.

nodes.label: S3A Connector (hadoop-aws)
nodes.description: S3AFileSystem (10 PRs), S3A input/output streams, credential providers, and integration tests. 25 PRs for S3 compatibility, performance (prefetch, magic committer), and ABFS-style test patterns.

nodes.label: Azure Connector (hadoop-azure)
nodes.description: ABFS filesystem implementation, rename recovery, metadata handling, and Azure-specific integration tests. 12 PRs for rename failure recovery, test timeout hardening, and listFiles fixes.

nodes.label: Other Hadoop Tools
nodes.description: DistCp, SLS (scheduler load simulator), Aliyun OSS connector, federation balance, and dynamometer. 20 PRs for tool maintenance and improvements.

nodes.label: Build System
nodes.description: Root Maven POM and submodule POMs managing dependencies, plugins, and profiles. 10 PRs for dependency updates and build configuration.

edges.label: HDFS core depends on common IPC, security, and filesystem APIs
HDFS client uses common configuration and RPC framework
HDFS server implements client protocol interfaces
RBF router delegates to HDFS namenode RPC interfaces
YARN common uses hadoop-common service and security framework
ResourceManager builds on YARN common abstractions
NodeManager uses YARN common resource and service APIs
YARN router federates across ResourceManager instances
MapReduce uses common FileSystem, IPC, and configuration
native client mirrors Java client protocol for C++ access
timeline and auxiliary servers use YARN common service model
YARN applications use common client and resource APIs
root POM defines dependency versions for all modules
